page: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor operating at 2.4 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not feature any GPU acceleration. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the m4.xlarge in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute resources. In stress-ng tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% category, with scores of 1282.39 and 3353.46 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests also reflect this positioning, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 177.74 GB/sec and uncached large block read/write speeds hovering between 21.65 GB/sec and 24.01 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET operations show weak to poor performance, with Redis achieving 1.44 million operations per second, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested instances.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m4.xlarge represents an older hardware generation. While it offers a balanced resource allocation profile, its lower computational density and weak benchmark results relative to newer architectures make it less suitable for compute-intensive tasks. The lack of local NVMe storage and limited 1 Gbps network baseline are notable tradeoffs. This instance is best utilized for legacy general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, development environments, or lightweight background tasks that do not require high-throughput processing or modern instruction sets like AVX-512.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.4 GHz on the Xen hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results consistently place this instance in the bottom 25% for CPU and memory performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 3353.46 and Redis SET operations falling into the bottom 10% at 1.44 million operations per second. Given these performance characteristics and its older hardware generation, the m4.xlarge is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and development environments where high-throughput computational power is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 CPU. It offers baseline performance for legacy workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor.
tagline: A legacy general-purpose instance offering balanced memory-to-CPU ratios for lightweight workloads and development environments without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Xen hypervisor-based x86_64 architecture with hyper-threading
Benchmark results place CPU and memory performance in the bottom 25%
Suited for lightweight general-purpose tasks and development environments
